calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-16-2023 Vacated

DEFENDANT HIGH POINT HOLDINGS LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS FOR PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH COURT ORDER.(FOR TENTATIVE RULING ONLY PART 2 OF 2)

Off Calendar Nov-06-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Plaintiff'S Failure To Comply With Court Order
Off Calendar Nov-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HIGH POINT HOLDINGS LLC'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Plaintiff'S Failure To Comply With Court Order

Defendants' unopposed Motion for Terminating Sanctions for Plaintiff's Failure to Comply With Court Order is GRANTED. A party's failure to oppose a motion may be deemed a consent to the granting of the motion. (Cal. R. Ct. 8.54(c).) In addition, On August 22, 2023, Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman adopted the tentative rulings with respect to each motion and granted them in their entirety. Plaintiff was ordered to provide written discovery responses by no later than August 31, 2023, with all objections waived. Plaintiff was also ordered to pay sanctions in the amount of $400.00 per motion, for a total of $1,200.00. Despite this Court's Orders to do so, Plaintiff has not provided Defendant with any written discovery responses and has not paid any sanctions. Civil Code Section 2023.030(d) states in pertinent part that "The Court may impose a terminating sanction by one of the following orders (3) An order dismissing the action, or any part of the action." Terminating sanctions may be imposed for misuse of the discovery process. (Code of Civil Procedure Section 2023.030(d); Sole Energy Co. v. Hodges, (2005) 128 Cal. App. 4th 199, 207). Plaintiff's failure to respond to Defendant's written discovery is a misuse of the discovery process; as is disobeying a court order to provide discovery. (Code of Civil Procedure Section 2023.010(d),(g)). The case is
dismissed and Judgment may be entered in favor of Defendant.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at pvanzandt@aghwlaw.com. The submitted proposed order is to show compliance with CRC 3.1312

Judge Pro Tem Peter Van Zandt: Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Plaintiff'S Failure To Comply With Court Order

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-22-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT HIGH POINT HOLDINGS LLC's Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; And Request For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's Motion to compel GRANTED. No timely opposition filed. Complete responses due no later than August 31, 2023, unless otherwise agreed by the parties. Objections are waived. Sanctions GRANTED in the amount of $400 for 2 hours to prepare the motion, payable at the same time as responses. Sanctions are STAYED and will be WAIVED if Plaintiff provides complete responses without objections by the date indicated.

Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
